subject: Fix sonar issues in integrity audit
commit_message:
Fix sonar issues in integrity audit

Mostly used Eclipse Refactor->Extract Method to reduce cyclomatic
complexity.  Also combined a few "if" statements to reduce nesting
levels.
Also addressed some sonar issues in the tests (e.g., use "<>" where
appropriate).
Did not attempt to increase junit coverage.

Change-Id: I9d6c1305ce455f0d64249b548d123bb9bf37292a
Issue-ID: POLICY-1791
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>

integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and drools-applications care about?

Patch Set 5:

> integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
 > drools-applications care about?

 > integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
 > drools-applications care about?

The features that use this functionality are disabled in the PDP-D side.
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 5:

> integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
 > drools-applications care about?

AFAIK, yes

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
>  > drools-applications care about?
> 
>  > integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
>  > drools-applications care about?
> 
> The features that use this functionality are disabled in the PDP-D side.

Ok, just wondering if these should be moved from policy/common to drools-applications?? Not sure if the other components would need these??

Patch Set 5:

> > Patch Set 5:
 > >
 > > > integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
 > >  > drools-applications care about?
 > >
 > >  > integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
 > >  > drools-applications care about?
 > >
 > > The features that use this functionality are disabled in the
 > PDP-D side.
 > 
 > Ok, just wondering if these should be moved from policy/common to
 > drools-applications?? Not sure if the other components would need
 > these??

I think the reason it is there is because policy/engine uses this in some capability.

 > > Patch Set 5:
 > >
 > > > integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
 > >  > drools-applications care about?
 > >
 > >  > integrity-audit: is this something that only drools and
 > >  > drools-applications care about?
 > >
 > > The features that use this functionality are disabled in the
 > PDP-D side.
 > 
 > Ok, just wondering if these should be moved from policy/common to
 > drools-applications?? Not sure if the other components would need
 > these??

The issue is that policy/engine uses it, so will have to keep it here until policy/engine deprecates.
I don't think other components will use it, even in default onap configuration, I don't see a good reason to enable it, but giving the choice to clients to use it in their customized deployments.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+2

Ok I thought later that maybe policy/engine uses it. thanks!!
Change has been successfully merged by Pamela Dragosh

Patch Set 5:

> Ok I thought later that maybe policy/engine uses it. thanks!!

You are correct - policy/engine uses both integrity-audit and integrity-monitor.
